Speaking: Read Aloud (RA)
RA 01
Yellow is considered as the most optimistic color. Yet surprisingly, people lose their tempers more often in yellow rooms and babies cry more in them. The reason may be that yellow is the hardest color for eyes to take in. So it can be overpowering if overused.
RA 02
The elephant is the largest living land mammal. During evolution, its skeleton has greatly altered from the actual mammal, designed for two main reasons. One is to cope with the great weight of huge grinding cheek teeth and elongated tusks making the jaw particularly massive. The other is to support the enormous bulk of such a huge body.
RA 03
Teenage girls are continuing to outperform boys in English while the gender gap in achievements in math and science has almost disappeared. The figures show that last year 80% of 14-year-old girls reached at least the expected level 5 in English, compared with 65% of boys. But in math, the girls are just 1% ahead of boys, while in science the difference is 2%.
RA 04
Located at the heart of two world famous cities, Liverpool and London, Liverpool's excellence in teaching, learning and research, first-class facilities and outstanding support places the university in the top 1% of universities worldwide. The University of Liverpool will provide you with an inspiring student experience, in a diverse international community.
RA 05
Schools host parent teacher conferences four times a year and it is important for families to attend. This is your chance to meet with teachers and ask questions about your child's progress. It can be helpful to write down questions ahead of time.
RA 06
Hundreds of millions of American people eat fast food every day without giving it too much thought, unaware of the subtle and not so subtle ramifications of their purchases. They just grab their tray off the counter, find a table, take a seat, unwrap the paper, and dig in. The whole experience is transitory and soon forgotten.
RA 07
The best time to visit Canada is during fall months, when mild temperatures and vibrant fall foliage make scenic drives, hiking, and outdoor exploration especially enjoyable. Travel guides often highlight autumn-especially from late September through October-as a peak season for color changes in Ontario, Quebec, and other regions of the country.
Speaking: Repeat Sentence (RS)
RS 01 [Ref: 130]
We are committed to attracting the very best students regardless of their financial circumstances.

Your boss requires you to finish your report before Friday.

The Best Time to visit Canada is fall months.

The train will not stop at the southern station due to the construction.

You can choose salad or soup to your meal.
RS 07 [Ref: NEW]
The computer lab is opened 24 hours a day.
RS 08 [Ref: NEW]
You can choose salad or soup to your meal.

If I miss the call, please record the message in the office.

If you give me your phone number, I can text you the address.

Some employees go to park for running in lunchtime.

I have to get up early for an interview tomorrow.

The library will be closed during summer vacation.

The wages must be paid into your bank account on Thursday.

Prepare the report for the manager.

The hotel is not far away from my apartment.

Please keep the keys with you because the front door often locks automatically.

If the doctor calls while I'm out, please leave a message.

Stand in line, and we will enter the library from the left side.

You can speak to the current students at the information sessions.

The test shows that you know a lot about Canada.

Speaking: Describe Image (DI)
Image Guide 1: Global Cat Ownership Share
The bar graph gives the information regarding which countries have the most cat owners. The data has been given in two percentages: 59, 43, 41, 40, 39, 36, 33, 32, 26, 19, 18, 16, and 9% in different countries: Russia, United States, Argentina, Italy, France, Vietnam, Brazil, Thailand, United Kingdom, Germany, Turkey, India, China, Japan, South Korea. By casting a casual glance, it can be clearly seen that the highest is 59%, lowest is 9%. All in all, the image provides crucial information regarding the topic.
Image Guide 2: Smartphone Integration Curve
The line graph gives the information regarding the rise of the smartphone. The data has been given in two percentages, 20, 40, 60, 80, and 100, in different categories: any cell phone, smartphone, in different years 2005, 2010, 2015, 2020. By casting a casual glance, it can be clearly seen that highest is 100% and the lowest is 40%. All in all, the image provides crucial information regarding the topic.
Image Guide 3: Regional Travel Time Metrics
The pie chart gives the information regarding travel time to work on various. The data has been given into percentage: 24.0, 32.2, 21.3, 10.1, 12.4 in different categories: less than 15 minutes, 15 to 29 minutes, 30 to 44 minutes, 45 to 59 minutes, more than one hour. By testing a casual glance, it can be clearly seen that the highest is 32.2%, the lowest is 10.1%. All in all, the image provides crucial information regarding the topic.
Image Guide 4: Meteorological Rainfall Volumes
The bar graph gives the information regarding average rainfall. The data has been given in two inches, 0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 47.25, 33.70, 7.66, 22.02. In different cities, New York, Dallas, Phoenix, Honolulu. By casting a casual glance, it can be clearly seen that highest is 47.25 inches, lowest is 7.66 inches. All in all, the image provides crucial information regarding the topic.
Image Guide 5: National Pet Domestic Scale
The bar graph gives the information regarding number of US households keeping pets. The data has been given into million: 84.9, 63.4, 42.7, 11.5, 5.7, 4.5, 1.6, 5.4 in different categories: total, dogs, cats, freshwater fish, birds, reptiles, horses, saltwater fish, other. By casting a casual glance, it can be clearly seen that the highest is 84.9 million and the lowest is 1.6 million. All in all, the image provides crucial information regarding the topic.
Image Guide 6: Academic Supplier Selection Interaction
This image shows a mother and her daughter shopping for school supplies. In the first part of the image, the mother is wearing a bright pink sweater and smiling at her daughter. The little girl is wearing a white sweater and holding a colorful school bag. They look happy while choosing the bag. There are many school bags and supplies on the shelves behind them. In the second part of the image, the girl is standing alone in the store. She has a big smile and is holding colorful pencils in her hands. She is also wearing a pink and white backpack. The background shows shelves full of pens, pencils, and other school items. She looks excited and ready for school. This image shows a sweet moment between a mother and her daughter. It also shows how children feel happy while getting new school supplies.
Speaking: Responding to a Situation (RTS)
RTS 01
You are planning to be away from home for three days and need someone to take care of your cat. You decide to ask your friend Monica for help. How would you explain the situation and request her assistance in feeding your cat while you are away?
Sample Answer:
Hi Monica, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. You are planning to be away from home for three days and need you to take care of your cat. I really realize the importance of this situation and I am hopeful for a positive outcome. Thank you for Understanding.
RTS 02
You are living in the same building with your colleague Mike, and you want to go to work with him every day and share the cost together. What would you say to him?
Sample Answer:
Hi John, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. I am living in the same building with you, and I want to go to work with you every day and share the cost together. I hope you would love this idea. This will be beneficial for us. We will really enjoy it. Thank you for Understanding.
RTS 03
You have a dinner date with a friend, and she wants to go to Sam's Cafe which only serves coffee, tea, etc. You want to eat in a restaurant with a lot of food. What do you say to your friend?
Sample Answer:
Hi Lorry, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. Lorry, you want to go to Sam's cafe which only serves coffee, tea, I want to eat in a restaurant with a lot of food. I hope you would love this idea. This will be beneficial for us. We will really enjoy it. Thank you for Understanding.
RTS 04
Your manager Joe is about to retire and he is going to travel in another country. You want to give him a farewell gift. How should you discuss with your colleagues what gift to prepare for the manager?
Sample Answer:
Hi Guys, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. Our manager Joe is about to retire and he is going to travel in another country. You want to give him a farewell gift. I hope you would love this idea. This will be beneficial for him. He will really enjoy it. Thank you for Understanding.
RTS 05
You are preparing dinner and your friend is calling you to discuss her daughter's problem. You really want to help your friend to solve her problem, but you are busy now, so you hope your friend could call you later. What do you say to your friend?
Sample Answer:
Hi Sarah, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. I am preparing dinner and you are calling me to discuss your daughter's problem. I really want to help you to solve your problem, but I am busy now. I assure you that this will not repeat again and I would be more careful in the future. Thank you for Understanding.
RTS 06
Your friend Susan suggests having a walk in the countryside tomorrow. You would love to, but you have seen the weather forecast and it might rain tomorrow. You want to wait and see the weather tomorrow morning, and decide then. What would you say to Susan right now?
Sample Answer:
Hi Susan, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. You suggest having a walk in the countryside tomorrow. I would love to, but I have seen the weather forecast and it might rain tomorrow. I want to wait and see the weather tomorrow morning, and decide then. This will be beneficial for us. Thank you for Understanding.
RTS 07
You are thirty minutes late for your doctor's appointment at 10 am. You need to explain to the receptionist and ask if the doctor could see you later today. What would you say to the receptionist?
Sample Answer:
Hi Manager, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. I am thirty minutes late for your doctor's appointment at 10 am. I need to explain to the receptionist and ask if the doctor could see me later today. I assure you that this will not repeat again. Thank you for Understanding.
RTS 08
You previously called the taxi driver and informed him that you were going to the airport, but now you have to change your destination to go home due to the flight cancel. You want to tell the driver where your home address is and how to get there. What should you say to the driver?
Sample Answer:
Hi Sarah, I hope you are doing well. I want to share about a situation in which I'm at this moment. I previously called the taxi driver and informed you that I was going to the airport, but now I have to change my destination to go home due to the flight cancel. I assure you that this will not repeat again. Thank you for Understanding.
Speaking: Answer Short Question (ASQ)
ASQ 01. What do you call a person who lives next to your house?
Answer: Neighbour
ASQ 02. What do we call a person undergoing treatments in a hospital?
Answer: Patient
ASQ 03. What geometric shape are circumference, diameter and radius related with?
Answer: Circle
ASQ 04. What is breakfast, lunch and dinner categorized as?
Answer: Meals
ASQ 05. What do we call a person who plays a sport, especially as a professional?
Answer: Athlete
ASQ 06. What item does one use to dry their body after a shower?
Answer: Towel
ASQ 07. What's the grey thing in the sky when it's about to rain?
Answer: Cloud
ASQ 08. What shows dishes before you order?
Answer: Menu
ASQ 09. What do you call a person who writes poems?
Answer: Poet
ASQ 10. What do we call a person who plays a sport, especially as a professional?
Answer: Athlete
ASQ 11. Which item of cutlery has several prongs usually three?
Answer: fork
ASQ 12. What's the grey thing in the sky when it's about to rain?
Answer: Cloud
ASQ 13 [NEW]. What do we call the first paragraph of a report?
Answer: Introduction
ASQ 14. What device can be used to take a photo?
Answer: Camera
ASQ 15. What do we call a person who writes a book?
Answer: Author/writer
ASQ 16. What clothing do people wear, such as students or nurses, to show that they belong to the same organizations?
Answer: Uniform
ASQ 17 [NEW]. What kind of soup utensils do you use at table?
Answer: Spoon
ASQ 18 [NEW]. What do people usually used to cut food in the plate?
Answer: Knife
ASQ 19. A long, orange vegetable known for being eaten by rabbits is called?
Answer: Carrot
ASQ 20. What do we call a part you take from a bread or a portion of cake?
Answer: Slice
Writing: Formal Task Responses
To:Parents of Enna's Classmates
Subject:Invitation to Enna's Birthday Party 🎉
Sample Answer

Dear Parents,

I'm thrilled to invite you and your children to Enna's birthday party on Saturday, September 14th, at 3:00 PM. The celebration will be held at 123 Elm Street, Springfield.

We have a delicious spread planned, including pizza, sandwiches, fruit platters, and a variety of snacks and desserts to enjoy throughout the event.

The party will feature a treasure hunt, exciting games, and a craft station where you can create your own party hats. We'll also have a photo booth set up to capture all the fun moments and memories.

We are excited to celebrate with you and make this day truly special for Enna.

Best regards,
Alex Thompson

To:Dani
Subject:Vacation Notice – October 7 to 18
Sample Answer

Dear Dani,

I hope you're doing well. I wanted to inform you that I will be on vacation from Monday, October 7th to Friday, October 18th.

I am taking this time off to attend to personal matters and recharge. During my absence, Sarah Davis will be covering my responsibilities. She is well-informed about ongoing projects and will handle any issues that may arise. You can reach Sarah at sarah.davis@email.com or (555) 123-4567 for any work-related concerns.

If an urgent matter requires my attention, I will be available via email at myemail@example.com. Please note that I will check my email less frequently.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
Alex Thompson

To:Oscar's Kitchen Team
Subject:Group Reservation Request – Birthday Celebration
Sample Answer

Dear Oscar's Kitchen Team,

I hope this message finds you well. I would like to make a group reservation for 20 people at your restaurant on Friday, September 13th. We would prefer to dine at 7:00 PM.

This booking is to celebrate my friend's birthday, and we are all excited about enjoying a meal at your establishment.

Could you please provide details on parking options available for our group? We want to ensure that everyone will have a convenient place to park.

Thank you for accommodating our request. Please let me know if you need any additional information or if there are any special arrangements we need to make.

Best regards,
Jamie Lee

To:Emily
Subject:Re: Help with Moving Furniture
Sample Answer

Dear Emily,

Thank you so much for inviting me to help with moving the furniture. I truly appreciate the thought and would have loved to assist.

I'm really sorry, due to work commitments, I am unable to be there. Additionally, I have limited transportation options at the moment and have pressing family responsibilities that require my attention.

I am really sorry I can't be there to help in person. However, if there are other ways I can support you, such as helping with packing or offering to coordinate with someone else who might assist, please let me know.

Thanks again for understanding, and best of luck with the move!

Best regards,
Jordan Lee

To:Ms. Susan
Subject:Re: Movie Invitation
Sample Answer

Dear Ms. Susan,

Thank you so much for inviting me to the movie. I really appreciate your kind invitation.

Unfortunately, I have to decline as I recently lost my bag, which contained my wallet and essential items. This unexpected situation has caused a few complications, and I need to sort things out urgently.

I am really sorry for not being able to attend. Your invitation means a lot to me, and I regret that I can't be there.

I would still love to watch a movie with you! Would you be free on Thursday or Saturday evening instead? Let me know what works best for you.

Looking forward to spending time together soon.

Best regards,
Alex Thompson

To:Mrs. Singh
Subject:Ideas for the Sports Club Party
Sample Answer

Mrs. Singh,

I hope you're doing well. As we prepare for the upcoming sports club party, I'd like to suggest a few ideas to make it lively and memorable.

For music, we could create an upbeat playlist or hire a local DJ who can take requests and keep the atmosphere energetic throughout the event.

For food, offering a mix of finger foods, sandwiches, and vegetarian options would cater to different preferences. A food truck or catering service could add variety and convenience.

We could also include activities such as quiz games, relay races, or a mini-tournament to encourage interaction and team spirit.

Thanks for considering these ideas. I'm excited for a great event!

Best regards,
Alex Thompson

To:Mr. Thompson
Subject:Concern About Lighting in the Building
Sample Answer

Dear Mr. Thompson,

I hope you are doing well. I am writing to share a concern about the lighting in our apartment building. I live on the second floor, and it is very dark at night.

There is only one small light at the entrance, and it does not give enough light. It is hard to see clearly, which can be unsafe.

There are also no lights near the stairs. This makes it difficult to walk up, especially when carrying bags or packages.

Could we please have brighter lights and install more along the stairs? Motion-sensing lights would also help and save electricity.

Thank you for your time and support.

Best regards,
Emily Carter
